design_philosophy.core_loop: Climb vines to collect numbers and arithmetic symbols, arrange them to solve math equations or reach target numbers set by Donkey Kong
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with spatial puzzle-solving; must navigate platforming while mentally solving arithmetic
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Educational mastery through familiar platforming IP; learning math through playful engagement with recognizable characters
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Aggressively educational design that prioritizes math instruction over gameplay enjoyment; reuses Donkey Kong Jr. engine and assets as a wrapper for math pedagogy rather than creating synergy between mechanics and learning. This polarizes reception—some see creative asset reuse, others see half-finished hybrid design.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Two game modes offer variable difficulty: Calculate mode is more open-ended and adaptive to player skill; +−×÷ Exercise mode presents fixed puzzles with predetermined solutions. Math difficulty can range from too easy for older children to too difficult/boring depending on age group.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression tied to math problem completion; Calculate mode repeats with incrementally higher target numbers; Exercise mode presents fixed problem sequences
replayability_hooks: Two distinct game modes (Calculate vs. Exercise)
Adjustable difficulty through math problem selection

world_building_techniques: Character-driven instruction: Donkey Kong holds up numbers as game state objective
Familiar IP context: Uses established Donkey Kong Jr. platformer framing to contextualize math problems

danger_signals: Educational game dressed in action-game clothing creates dissonance with target audience expectations
Pedagogical mismatch: math problems either trivialize learning or bore children; fails pedagogical spectrum
environmental_storytelling: Donkey Kong's presence as number-holder frames father-son relationship within education narrative
Vine-climbing mechanic borrowed from platformer context but repurposed for arithmetic navigation

sources_used: Wikipedia - Donkey Kong Jr. Math (gameplay modes, design philosophy, reception analysis)
The Cutting Room Floor - Donkey Kong Jr. Math (technical development, asset reuse documentation)
